blocks.content: Amy briefly recounted the story of the Ice Queen.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This is the Ice Queen. She's as beautiful as the rumors say.
800 years ago, it was a time when mages were branded as witches.
In a way, she was a tragic woman.
The Ice Queen was originally a mage.
At that time, the era was dominated by theocracy, so she hid her identity, but there was no way to hide her beautiful appearance.
When the woman's beauty spread by word of mouth and reached the royal palace, the old king summoned her.
Enchanted at first sight, the king made her his queen.
However, having lived her life pursued by the Holy Order, darkness had already grown in her heart.
Hundreds of innocent people were subjected to horrific torture and killed under the pretext of magical experiments.
As more people died, the queen's magical power grew stronger.
As her power grew, the queen went mad.
It is extremely rare for a mage to go mad, as the profession itself strengthens the mind.
However, if the mind is destroyed for any reason, it results in a situation far more severe than ordinary mental illness.
As the mind and magic become intertwined, the mage loses control over their magical power.
Mages call this phenomenon 'magical synchronization.'
The queen, consumed by madness, froze everything around her.
Her room, adorned with icicles, resembled an ice cave in the Arctic.
Not only the furniture like the bed and dressing table but also the maids and servants who attended to her were frozen to death.
As the situation spiraled out of control, the king built a small castle in the Les Mountains and made her the queen of that place.
It was essentially an exile.
But even there, the queen's bizarre behavior did not stop.
The first subjects of her experiments were dozens of attendants.
When they all died, she captured commoners and continued her cruel experiments.
After a year, no one was left in the small castle in the Les Mountains.
But the queen was satisfied.
She had finally reached the level she desired after long experiments.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I will sleep in a place where even time is frozen. When I return, the world will kneel at my feet.
The queen cast her final spell, encasing herself in a massive block of ice.
This was the story behind the last appearance of the Ice Queen that Shirone was looking at.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, I see.
Shirone moved a little closer to the Ice Queen.
Hearing the story made it feel more real.
The figure with closed eyes in hibernation truly seemed like someone from 800 years ago.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Perhaps she was tormented by guilt even as her mind was destroyed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's why she devoted herself to a spell that would freeze even herself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That could be.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it's true, it's really impressive.
A mage cannot die from their own spell.
While not theoretically impossible, casting a spell that harms oneself requires a will that transcends omnipotence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If the Spirit Zone wavers, the spell won't activate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ordinary mages can't even attempt it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, maybe it's closely related to the phenomenon of magical alignment?
Shirone stroked his chin, lost in thought.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why so serious? It's just a legend anyway.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's not even clear if the Ice Queen was a real person.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: And the figure in the ice is just a wax doll.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But it really looks real.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy also acknowledged the doll's craftsmanship.
It was only natural that they put effort into the festival's main attraction.
Shirone and Amy moved closer to examine the figure of the queen encased in ice.
Ahhh!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is that!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Idiot! There's a mechanism on the floor.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: If you step on it, the doll's eyes open.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who knew?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And you were just as scared.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who was scared?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It was your loud voice that startled me.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Men are such cowards.
Shirone pointed at Amy's pale face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! For someone who wasn't scared, your face is pretty pale.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Oh, really?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Should I make your face even paler?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: If you throw up your guts, it'll look like thin soup.
As Amy approached, rubbing her fist, Shirone averted his gaze and mumbled while looking at the floor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If you were scared, just admit it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You're not a gangster...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Gangster?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah, I'm a gangster.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are you going to do about it?
